name: BOL-148

dosing: ~3 mg per person (referenced by Dr. Halpern / Entheogen)
Equivalent to many multiples of a standard LSD trip dose (non-hallucinogenic at any dose)
Unspecified (general references to clinical use, no personal dosing data available)
preparations: Research-grade compound from THC Pharm (Germany), not available to individuals
Synthesized by adding bromine atom to LSD at R2 position
No preparation data from personal use (substance never publicly available)
schedule: 3 doses over 15 days, then 1 booster at 6 months (Karst/Hannover trial)
Clinical administration under medical supervision only (no self-dosing protocols exist)
Proposed Phase II trial: 10 centers in Germany and Austria (never completed)
Hoped-for future prescription use as preventative (not daily, used rarely)

sideEffects: Non-hallucinogenic / no psychedelic effects (primary distinguishing feature from LSD)
No significant side effects reported in Hannover trial
Cannot treat PTSD (psychedelic experience required for PTSD benefit)
Less effective for migraine than LSD or methysergide (Sicuteri 1963 data)
coTreatments: RC seeds / LSA (most accessible legal alternative)
